steps.title: Identify the specific bias Odom et al. diagnose
steps.content: The key signal is Odom's direct explanation of the historical mischaracterization: research was concentrated “near universities in the temperate northern hemisphere, where female birdsong is less common than it is in the tropics.” Odom et al. attribute the problem to geographic selection bias in research locations, not to study methods.

steps.title: Apply that diagnosis to the answer choices
steps.content:
The geographic location bias that Odom et al. identify is captured by C: the mischaracterization arose from “the kinds of study sites researchers tended to select.” A, B, and D point to different causes—evolutionary drivers, regional purpose differences, or researcher sex bias—that Odom never invokes.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
